title: Timber certification schemes (Category A evidence): methodology for review
body: "Companies supplying timber and wood-derived products to government must provide evidence that they meet the required standard for sustainability and legality.   One acceptable form of evidence is compliance with an acceptable timber certification scheme (Category A evidence). UK government has developed a set of criteria to assess which timber certifications schemes are acceptable. The methodology used to assess this was updated in February 2025.   Evidence of an independently verifiable legal and sustainable timber source can also be supplied through:     a Forest Law Enforcement, Governance and Trade (FLEGT) licence     other forms of credible evidence other than timber certification schemes – this is known as Category B evidence   "
